item.dc:creator: Nicole Acevedo
Carmen Sesin
Ryan J. Reilly
Gary Grumbach
Orlando Matos
item.description: The Justice Department indicted former Cuban president Raúl Castro on Wednesday in connection to the 1996 shooting of two civilian planes that killed four Cuban Americans.

media:content.media:text: Raul Castro
media:content.media:description: Raul Castro in Havana on May 1, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Photos of Brothers to the Rescue pilots Carlos Costa, Amando Alejandre Jr., Mario de la Peña and Pablo Morales.
media:content.media:description: Photos of Brothers to the Rescue pilots Carlos Costa, Amando Alejandre Jr., Mario de la Peña and Pablo Morales displayed at Florida International University.

media:content.media:text: Cuba Brothers to the Rescue.
media:content.media:description: Some of the boats from the flotilla head for open waters in 1996 off Key West, Fla., carrying Cuban Americans to the site of the downing of two Brothers to the Rescue planes by Cuban fighters.

item.title: DOJ indicts Raúl Castro over fatal 1996 civilian planes’ shooting

item.description: The American doctor who tested positive for Ebola while working in Congo has been evacuated to Berlin to receive emergency care. Health officials are racing to trace infections in Central Africa and worry the scale of the outbreak is already larger than first reported. NBC’s Molly Hunter reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Health Officials Race to Trace Rising Ebola Cases in Central Africa
media:content.media:description: The American doctor who tested positive for Ebola while working in Congo has been evacuated to Berlin to receive emergency care. Health officials are racing to trace infections in Central Africa and worry the scale of the outbreak is already larger than first reported. NBC’s Molly Hunter reports for TODAY.

item.title: Health Officials Race to Trace Rising Ebola Cases in Central Africa

item.dc:creator: Erik Ortiz
item.description: An NBC News analysis of final statements from prisoners on Texas’ death row shows that some convey a readiness to die. Others are defiant. Many seek salvation.

media:content.media:text: The last words of Texas' executed inmates are transcribed by hand and collected online.
media:content.media:description: The last words of Texas' executed inmates are transcribed by hand and collected online.

media:content.media:text: Jerome Butler, executed in 1990, was convicted in a 1986 shooting death.
media:content.media:description: The last words of Texas' executed inmates are transcribed by hand and collected online.

media:content.media:text: Charlie Livingston, executed in 1997, was convicted in a 1983 robbery and murder.
media:content.media:description: The last words of Texas' executed inmates are transcribed by hand and collected online.

media:content.media:text: Lawrence Buxton, executed in 1991, was convicted in a 1980 robbery and shooting death.
media:content.media:description: The last words of Texas' executed inmates are transcribed by hand and collected online.

media:content.media:text: Warren Bridge, executed in 1994, was convicted in a 1980 robbery and shooting death.
media:content.media:description: The last words of Texas' executed inmates are transcribed by hand and collected online.

item.title: After 5 decades and 600 executions, these are the last words on Texas’ death row

item.description: Navy veteran Ed Gallrein praised President Trump during his victory speech after defeating Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky's 4th Congressional District. Gallrein added that the winners are “the Republicans of Kentucky congressional district four and their families,” which he says he “will be a champion for in Washington."

media:content.media:title: Gallrein praises 'courageous' Trump in Kentucky primary victory speech
media:content.media:description: Navy veteran Ed Gallrein praised President Trump during his victory speech after defeating Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky's 4th Congressional District. Gallrein added that the winners are “the Republicans of Kentucky congressional district four and their families,” which he says he “will be a champion for in Washington."

item.title: Gallrein praises 'courageous' Trump in Kentucky primary victory speech

item.dc:creator: Henry J. Gomez
item.description: Former Navy SEAL Ed Gallrein has won the Republican primary in Kentucky’s 4th Congressional District over Rep. Thomas Massie.

media:content.media:text: Rep. Thomas Massie and challenger Ed Gallrein
media:content.media:description: Rep. Thomas Massie and challenger Ed Gallrein

item.title: Trump-endorsed Ed Gallrein unseats Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky GOP primary

item.description: Former Rep. Barney Frank, who was the first openly gay member of Congress and known for the Dodd-Frank Act, died at age 86. He had entered hospice care at his home in Maine in April and is survived by his husband of 14 years, Jim Ready.

media:content.media:title: Barney Frank, first openly gay member of Congress and Wall Street reformer, dies at 86
media:content.media:description: Former Rep. Barney Frank, who was the first openly gay member of Congress and known for the Dodd-Frank Act, died at age 86. He had entered hospice care at his home in Maine in April and is survived by his husband of 14 years, Jim Ready.

item.title: Barney Frank, first openly gay member of Congress and Wall Street reformer, dies at 86

item.description: The Trump administration is defending what it calls its “anti-weaponization” fund, using $1.8 billion of taxpayer money to pay Trump allies who say they were wrongly prosecuted by the Biden administration, and is not ruling out compensating convicted Jan. 6 rioters. On Capitol Hill, Democrats grilled acting Attorney General and former Trump personal lawyer Todd Blanche. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Trump’s $1.8B ‘Anti-Weaponization Fund’ Draws Outrage
media:content.media:description: The Trump administration is defending what it calls its “anti-weaponization” fund, using $1.8 billion of taxpayer money to pay Trump allies who say they were wrongly prosecuted by the Biden administration, and is not ruling out compensating convicted Jan. 6 rioters. On Capitol Hill, Democrats grilled acting Attorney General and former Trump personal lawyer Todd Blanche. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY.

item.title: Trump’s $1.8B ‘Anti-Weaponization Fund’ Draws Outrage

item.dc:creator: Gretchen Morgenson
item.description: Risks are mounting in the life insurance industry, and experts are concerned state regulators are not protecting policyholders.

media:content.media:text: A $100 dollar bill disappearing into red
media:content.media:description: The business of life insurance used to be boring. But the model has changed, with more aggressive insurers taking on more risks in their investments, research has shown.

media:content.media:text: Annie Benjamin
media:content.media:description: The business of life insurance used to be boring. But the model has changed, with more aggressive insurers taking on more risks in their investments, research has shown.

media:content.media:text: Image: Thomas D. Gober
media:content.media:description: The business of life insurance used to be boring. But the model has changed, with more aggressive insurers taking on more risks in their investments, research has shown.

item.title: She paid an insurance company $99,000 to generate retirement income for life. Then it collapsed.

item.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping welcomed Russian President Vladimir Putin to Beijing for a meeting one week after Xi met with President Donald Trump for a summit. NBC News' Janis Mackey Frayer reports on how China is seeking to project itself as a stabilizing force on the world stage.

media:content.media:title: China and Russia sign agreements strengthening their ties after Xi-Putin meeting
media:content.media:description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping welcomed Russian President Vladimir Putin to Beijing for a meeting one week after Xi met with President Donald Trump for a summit. NBC News' Janis Mackey Frayer reports on how China is seeking to project itself as a stabilizing force on the world stage.

item.title: China and Russia sign agreements strengthening their ties after Xi-Putin meeting

item.description: NBC News projects that Virginia voters have approved redrawing the congressional district map. NBC News' Steve Kornacki provides analysis of the vote.

media:content.media:title: Virginia voters approve congressional redistricting amendment
media:content.media:description: NBC News projects that Virginia voters have approved redrawing the congressional district map. NBC News' Steve Kornacki provides analysis of the vote.

item.title: Virginia voters approve congressional redistricting amendment

item.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

media:content.media:title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
media:content.media:description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

item.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio

item.dc:creator: Erik Ortiz
item.description: An NBC News analysis of final statements from prisoners on Texas’ death row shows that some convey a readiness to die. Others are defiant. Many seek salvation.

media:content.media:text: The last words of Texas' executed inmates are transcribed by hand and collected online.
media:content.media:description: The last words of Texas' executed inmates are transcribed by hand and collected online.

media:content.media:text: Jerome Butler, executed in 1990, was convicted in a 1986 shooting death.

media:content.media:text: Charlie Livingston, executed in 1997, was convicted in a 1983 robbery and murder.

media:content.media:text: Lawrence Buxton, executed in 1991, was convicted in a 1980 robbery and shooting death.
media:content.media:description: The last words of Texas' executed inmates are transcribed by hand and collected online.

item.dc:creator: Berkeley Lovelace Jr.
item.description: A vaccine for the Ebola strain driving the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda is likely months away from human trials, and there is no guarantee it would work, the World Health Organization said Wednesday.

media:content.media:text: A staff member of the CBCA Virunga Hospital checks a visitor's temperature
media:content.media:description: A staff member of the CBCA Virunga Hospital in Goma, Congo, checks a visitor's temperature.

item.title: Ebola vaccine for Bundibugyo strain could take months before human trials

item.dc:creator: Steve Kopack
item.description: Amazon executive chairman Jeff Bezos accused U.S. politicians of villainizing the ultra-wealthy and using tax policy as a political wedge issue.

media:content.media:text: Jeff Bezos.
media:content.media:description: Jeff Bezos, founder of Amazon.com Inc., in Miami in 2025.

item.title: Jeff Bezos says raising taxes on the wealthy won’t help the average American

item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke
item.description: A class action lawsuit has been filed against The Walt Disney Co.

media:content.media:text: Anaheim Exteriors And Landmarks - 2023
media:content.media:description: Disneyland in Anaheim, Calif. in 2023.

item.title: Disney sued over facial recognition technology at California parks

item.dc:creator: Matt Lavietes
item.description: A TikTok star was arrested in California this week and accused of trying to hire someone to kill her daughter’s father, boy band singer Jack Avery

media:content.media:text: Infulencer Gabbie Gonazales; singer Jack Avery.
media:content.media:description: Jack Avery of Why Don't We performs in 2019.

media:content.media:text: Influencer Gabbie Gonazalez; singer Jack Avery.
media:content.media:description: Jack Avery of Why Don't We performs in 2019.

media:content.media:text: 2022 iHeartRadio Music Awards - Arrivals
media:content.media:description: Jack Avery of Why Don't We performs in 2019.

item.title: A TikTok star recruited her dad to kill her boy band baby daddy, officials say

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
item.description: Wednesday is another sweltering day for those in the mid-Atlantic as temperatures soar into the mid-90s once again just before a storm front is set to take over the Eastern Seaboard

media:content.media:text: APTOPIX US Severe Weather New York
media:content.media:description: A woman uses a fan in Times Square during a heat advisory in New York, Tuesday, May 19, 2026.

media:content.media:text: California Wildfires
media:content.media:description: A firefighter works to control the Sandy Fire in Simi Valley, Calif., on Tuesday.

item.title: Extreme heat lingers across the mid-Atlantic, but storms are expected to cool off states

item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly
item.description: Jan. 6 officers sue over $1.8B pot they call ‘slush fund’ for ‘insurrectionists’

media:content.media:text: Daniel Hodges, MPD officer, left, and Harry Dunn, U.S. Capitol Police officer, listen during a hearing on the Jan. 6 attack at the Capitol on June 21, 2022.
media:content.media:description: Metropolitan Police Department Officer Daniel Hodges, left, and U.S. Capitol Police Office Harry Dunn listen during a Jan. 6 committee hearing at the Capitol on June 21, 2022.

media:content.media:text: Rioters and police inside the Capitol
media:content.media:description: Metropolitan Police Department Officer Daniel Hodges, left, and U.S. Capitol Police Office Harry Dunn listen during a Jan. 6 committee hearing at the Capitol on June 21, 2022.

item.title: Jan. 6 officers sue over $1.8B pot they call ‘slush fund’ for ‘insurrectionists’

Alexander Smith
Erika Edwards
Abigail Williams
item.description: Until last year, the U.S.

media:content.media:text: A border health officer at the Busunga crossing between Uganda and the Democratic Republic of Congo checks a traveler's temperature.
media:content.media:description: A border health officer at the crossing between Uganda and the Democratic Republic of Congo checks a traveler's temperature Monday.

media:content.media:text: A general view of Kyeshero Hospital in Goma.
media:content.media:description: A border health officer at the crossing between Uganda and the Democratic Republic of Congo checks a traveler's temperature Monday.

media:content.media:description: A border health officer at the crossing between Uganda and the Democratic Republic of Congo checks a traveler's temperature Monday.

item.title: Absence of USAID likely slowed Ebola detection and response, former officials say

item.dc:creator: Daniel Arkin
item.description: Barney Frank, the quick-witted Massachusetts congressman and liberal lion who helped overhaul Wall Street regulations after the 2008 financial crisis and made history as one of the first openly gay members of Congress, died Wednesday, his sister confirmed to NBC Boston

media:content.media:text: Rep. Barnett "Barney" Frank in 1991.
media:content.media:description: Rep. Barnett "Barney" Frank in 1991.

media:content.media:text: Barney Frank, Mayor Kevin White's Assistant
media:content.media:description: Frank was Boston Mayor Kevin White's assistant in 1968.

media:content.media:text: Clinton State Of The Union Address 1999
media:content.media:description: Congressman Barney Frank surrounded by fellow lawmakers during President Bill Clinton's State of the Union address in 1999.

media:content.media:text: Barney Frank and Nancy Pelosi.
media:content.media:description: Barney Frank and Nancy Pelosi in 2008 in Washington, D.C.

media:content.media:text: Jim Ready and Congressman Barney Frank at their home in Ogunquit Monday, July 9, 2012. The two were
media:content.media:description: Jim Ready and Frank at their home in Ogunquit, Maine, following their wedding in 2012.

media:content.media:text: PFLAG National's Love Takes Justice event in Washington DC at AFT Headquarters
media:content.media:description: Frank speaks at PFLAG National's Love Takes Justice event in Washington, D.C., on Nov. 18, 2025.

item.title: Former Rep. Barney Frank, champion of Wall Street reform and gay rights trailblazer, dies at 86

item.dc:creator: Mithil Aggarwal
item.description: China-Russia relations are at a “historic high,” Chinese leader Xi Jinping said Wednesday, as he met with Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing days after hosting U.S. President Donald Trump.

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-CHINA-RUSSIA-POLITICS-DIPLOMACY
media:content.media:description: TOPSHOT - In this pool photograph distributed by the Russian state agency Sputnik, Russia's President Vladimir Putin (L) and China's President Xi Jinping take part in a welcome ceremony at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing on May 20, 2026.

media:content.media:text: CHINA-RUSSIA-POLITICS-DIPLOMACY
media:content.media:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin and Chinese leader Xi Jinping during a welcome ceremony at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing on Wednesday.

media:content.media:text: Putin, Xi meet in Beijing following Trump visit amid US-Iran war
media:content.media:description: Putin and Xi in Beijing on Wednesday.

item.title: Putin and Xi say ties are at ‘unprecedented’ high at friendly summit after Trump visit

item.dc:creator: Alexander Smith
item.description: The suspected death toll from the Ebola outbreak in central Africa rose again Wednesday, as a group of potentially exposed Americans were arriving in Europe for monitoring and the World Health Organization warned a vaccine was still months away

media:content.media:text: DRCONGO-HEALTH-VIRUS-EBOLA
media:content.media:description: FC/M23 soldiers provide security for the movement's authorities at the Rodolphe Mérieux Laboratory, National Biomedical Research Institute (INRB) in Goma, on May 19, 2026 during their guided visit to the laboratory responsible for analyzing and handling suspected Ebola cases.

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-DRCONGO-HEALTH-VIRUS-EBOLA
media:content.media:description: A health worker monitors visitors arriving at the National Biomedical Research Institute in Goma, on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-DRCONGO-HEALTH-VIRUS-EBOLA
media:content.media:description: Workers take part in sanitation, renovation, and leveling work at the former Ebola treatment center, in Goma on Tuesday.

item.title: Ebola death toll rises as two Americans are flown to Europe for monitoring

item.dc:creator: Deanna Pai
item.description: Retinal is more potent and faster-acting than retinol, and I asked experts for the best picks from Youth to the People, Beauty of Joseon, Naturium and more.

media:content.media:text: Retinal can still cause irritation, so dermatologists recommend looking for fragrance-free versions with hydrating ingredients.
media:content.media:description: Retinal can still cause irritation, so dermatologists recommend looking for fragrance-free versions with hydrating ingredients.

media:content.media:text: Retinal can still cause irritation, so dermatologists recommend looking for fragrance-free versions with hydrating ingredients.
media:content.media:description: Retinal can still cause irritation, so dermatologists recommend looking for fragrance-free versions with hydrating ingredients.

item.title: Which is better — retinal or retinol?

item.dc:creator: Ashley Morris
item.description: I rounded up the best deals this week from Ulta, J. Crew, Home Depot and more on sunscreen, summer clothes, outdoor furniture and more.

media:content.media:text: You can save on bestsellers from Ulta, J. Crew, Ilia and more.
media:content.media:description: You can save on bestsellers from Ulta, J. Crew, Ilia and more.

media:content.media:text: You can save on bestsellers from Ulta, J. Crew, Ilia and more.
media:content.media:description: You can save on bestsellers from Ulta, J. Crew, Ilia and more.

item.title: 68+ best sales we plan to shop this week: Ilia, J. Crew and more
